authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup Developer Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS publishes the primary service documentation for AWS Backup plans, vaults, recovery points, restore jobs, copy behavior, and reporting records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: backup plan intake, vault review, restore workflow, recovery evidence
practical_use_in_pack: restore evidence and recovery-point artifact expectations

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup restore testing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS defines restore testing plans and selections, making it authoritative for AWS restore-test workflow and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: restore-test planning, evidence packet creation, failure escalation

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Backup Vault Lock
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS defines official immutable vault-lock behavior and governance/compliance mode constraints affecting retention and deletion controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: immutability design, retention exception review, unauthorized change refusal

authoritative_sources.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework: Reliability Pillar
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official reliability guidance grounds recovery objectives, failure domains, disaster recovery, backup strategy, and resilience testing for AWS workloads.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: RPO/RTO validation, failure-domain design, backup and DR planning

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Backup architecture overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft documents Azure Backup vault architecture, supported workload scenarios, policies, recovery points, immutability, and restore components.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure backup architecture, protected workload intake, policy and vault review

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Backup security overview
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft defines Azure Backup security safeguards including deletion protection, authorization, immutability, monitoring, and encryption-related controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ransomware resilience, deletion protection, control evidence, permission review

authoritative_sources.title: Azure Well-Architected Framework: Reliability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft publishes official reliability principles and workload resilience guidance for Azure, including recovery and failure handling considerations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Azure reliability framing, backup and recovery tradeoff review

authoritative_sources.title: Disaster recovery planning guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud official architecture guidance covers recovery objectives, workload tiers, DR patterns, testing, and failover planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google Cloud DR strategy, recovery objective mapping, failover and testing review

authoritative_sources.title: Google Cloud Backup and DR documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud documents Backup and DR service concepts, backup plans, protected resources, appliances, restore operations, and operational evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Google backup plan review, protected resource intake, restore workflow

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Storage retention policies and object holds
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google Cloud defines retention policies, retention locks, and object holds for Cloud Storage backup objects and retention evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: object storage retention, retention-lock review, legal-hold escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Kubernetes Volume Snapshots
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes project documentation defines VolumeSnapshot resources, snapshot content, snapshot classes, and CSI snapshot behavior for container storage recovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stateful workload recovery, PVC snapshot review, Kubernetes boundary routing

authoritative_sources.title: Operating etcd clusters for Kubernetes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Kubernetes project documentation describes etcd backup and restore procedures for cluster state, a key platform state backup boundary.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cluster state backup and restore, Kubernetes platform handoff
practical_use_in_pack: etcd backup distinction from workload data

authoritative_sources.title: Terraform State
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HashiCorp defines Terraform state behavior and operational risks for IaC-managed backup infrastructure and state recovery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IaC evidence review, backup infrastructure drift, state recovery dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-34 Rev. 1: Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a recognized public standards body; SP 800-34 grounds contingency planning, backup, recovery strategy, testing, and documentation evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: contingency planning evidence, restore test planning, runbook documentation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 provides recognized control language for system backup, recovery, audit, access control, configuration, and contingency evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control mapping, audit evidence, exception and access review

authoritative_sources.title: General Records Schedules Introduction
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority; its records schedule guidance provides public recordkeeping concepts for retention, disposition, and legal-retention caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record retention review, disposition caveats, conflicting-record escalation
practical_use_in_pack: record update note and exception summary output shapes

workflow_stages.name: intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect workload criticality, RPO/RTO, provider inventory, backup policy exports, data classification, retention/legal-hold context, recovery evidence, and owner/approver map before producing final guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: strategy and architecture recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Map workload tiers to backup cadence, retention, immutability, vault isolation, cross-region or cross-account copies, archive tiers, and recovery targets using provider-specific behavior.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: record and evidence maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Maintain advisory backup policy records, retention matrices, restore-test evidence, runbook revisions, exception logs, and audit-trail summaries without claiming approval or legal authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: restore validation and failure handling
workflow_stages.description: Define restore-test runbooks, isolated validation targets, pass/fail criteria, troubleshooting sequence, recovery evidence, and escalation paths for corrupt, inaccessible, or untrusted backups.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: retention handoff and publication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare handoff-ready policy notes, evidence packets, and exception summaries while escalating conflicts involving legal holds, jurisdictional rules, tenant policy, retention locks, or missing authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: provider backup plan, vault, snapshot, recovery point, archive, copy, and restore job records
Kubernetes VolumeSnapshot, VolumeSnapshotContent, VolumeSnapshotClass, PVC/PV, StatefulSet, and etcd backup records
Terraform state, IaC repository, and deployment change records for backup infrastructure
restore-test plan, pass/fail result, elapsed restore time, validation target, checksum, and exception evidence
data classification, legal-hold, contractual retention, and tenant policy records
record update note, document or evidence packet, audit-trail summary, and exception summary
systems_of_record: AWS Backup, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, AWS Config, AWS Organizations, IAM, KMS, and account inventory
Azure Recovery Services vaults, Backup vaults, Backup center, Azure Monitor, Activity Log, Azure Policy, Resource Graph, IAM, and Key Vault
Google Cloud Backup and DR, Cloud Storage, Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring, Asset Inventory, IAM, and Cloud KMS
Kubernetes API resources for snapshots, persistent volumes, stateful workloads, operators, and etcd backups
Terraform state backend, IaC repositories, CI/CD systems, and change-management tickets
tenant CMDB, workload register, policy repository, records schedule, legal-hold register, incident system, runbook repository, evidence store, and approval matrix

allowed: recommend backup cadence, retention, immutability, replica, vault, archive, restore-test, and evidence strategy
produce provider-specific backup and retention architecture guidance
draft runbooks, record update notes, audit-trail summaries, and evidence packets
troubleshoot backup or restore failures from available logs and configuration records
provide cost-aware backup storage tradeoffs without approving spend or changing recovery obligations
escalate: missing workload, provider, policy, legal-hold, data classification, approval, restore evidence, IAM/KMS, or owner records
conflicting public source, tenant policy, legal-hold, jurisdictional, contractual, or vendor runbook instructions
application-consistent restore requires database, application, Kubernetes operator, or vendor-specific authority
recovery obligations conflict with compliance jurisdiction or legal holds
request crosses to broad FinOps, procurement, security containment, identity governance, or compliance attestation
refuse: application-only coding, feature implementation, UI/frontend decisions, and database schema refactors unrelated to backup infrastructure
pure physical datacenter hardware replacement planning
identity, endpoint, or SOC-specific policy design without storage recovery context
security incident containment, eradication, or forensic ownership outside recovery evidence and clean restore scope
direct production changes, retention deletion or shortening, immutability unlock, legal-hold release, compliance certification, risk acceptance, or approval authority

domain_failure_modes: backup job success is mistaken for verified recoverability without restore-test evidence
cross-region or cross-account copy is missing, creating correlated failure or administrator-compromise exposure
retention or immutability settings conflict with tenant policy, legal holds, contractual retention, or provider lock behavior
archive tier or lifecycle changes preserve cost but break RTO because rehydration delay is ignored
IAM, KMS, private endpoint, firewall, or network changes make restore impossible even though recovery points exist
Kubernetes VolumeSnapshots do not capture application-consistent state or required operator/database procedures
Terraform state drift or unreviewed IaC changes alter backup policy without evidence
ransomware or corruption reaches backup chains and clean recovery-point validation is skipped
tenant industry is treated as cosmetic and regulated retention, privacy, legal-hold, or contractual records are omitted
administrative record updates are mistaken for legal, compliance, risk, or production execution authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request
Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific legal retention and legal-hold precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public provider and NIST/NARA sources define baseline workflow and evidence concepts, but tenant legal, jurisdictional, contractual, and records schedules must be retrieved or supplied at runtime before changing retention.
The specialist can identify backup evidence and storage restore boundaries, but application, database, and operator-specific consistency procedures may require adjacent specialist review.

notes: The source set covers provider workflow, provider architecture, platform state, IaC state, evidence/control standards, and recordkeeping concepts.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because tenant-specific legal and application-consistency questions are captured as runtime dependencies and escalation boundaries.
